sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jacqueline, presents with anxiety regarding her recent blood work showing hepatitis C.
She reports being surprised by this diagnosis, despite feeling fine.
She has a history of IV drug use in the past, but has been clean for over 15 years.
She also reports a history of heavy alcohol use, currently consuming about a beer a day, and smoking one to two cigarettes a day.
She denies any other medical conditions.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Her family history is significant for hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Review of systems: The patient reports fatigue at the end of the day, despite getting enough sleep, but denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appears in no distress and no jaundice on skin exam.
The heart exam reveals a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
The lungs are clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
The abdomen is soft with no hepatosplenomegaly, and bowel sounds are present.
The musculoskeletal exam is unremarkable.
Test Results: The patient's recent blood work shows a positive HCV antibody test and an elevated AST at 39, but normal ALT, albumin, and total bilirubin levels.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a confirmed diagnosis of hepatitis C, a viral infection affecting the liver.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; Suspected liver disease; Suspected liver fibrosis.
Plan:
I ordered additional blood work to confirm the diagnosis with HCV RNA and HCV genotype testing.
I also ordered a special ultrasound to determine the severity of the patient's liver disease and to assess for liver fibrosis.
I will use this information to guide treatment.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in about three weeks to review the results of the additional testing and to discuss further management.
I instructed the patient to schedule her family members for HCV screening.
